content.full_text:
PPC OPINIONS 
\ 
BEFORE THE FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
In the Matter of: 
) 
) 
Opinion requested by 
} 
Mayor Janet Gray Hayes) 
Dinner Con~ittee 
) 
-----------------------) 
A-
No. 75:.:.145 
December 4, 1975 
BY THE COMHISSION: 
We have been asked the follmving 
question by the Hayor Janet Gray Hayes Dinner Committee: 
The Mayor Janet Gray Hayes Dinner Co~~ittee spon-
sored a dinner on April 17, 1975, to raise funds for the 
non-reimbursable expenses of the Hayor's office. 
Seven 
hundred tickets were sold at $20 apiece and the proceeds 
will be used to hire a part-time assistant who will work 
to maintain the organii~tion which handled the Mayor's 
campaign, to pUQlish a newsletter, and to cover non-reim-
bursable travel, dinner and postage expenses, among other 
items. 
Do the above-described uses of the proceeds of 
such a fundraising dinner disqualify those proceeds from 
being treated solely as campaign contributions and expendi-
tures reportable under Chapter 4 of the Political Reform 
Act and make these proceeds "income" within the meaning of 
Section 82030, ,·,hich is reportable by the Hayor under Chap-
ter 7 of the Act? 
CONCLUSION 
The proceeds from the fundraising dinner retain 
their classification solely as campaign contributions and 
expenditures so long as they are used to support those 
activiLies L~lated to the Mayor's res~ons 
lities as an 
elected official and a future candidate. 


• 
No. 75-145 
Page Two 
1 FPPC OPINIONS 
ANALYSIS 
We are concerned here with whether funds received 
by an elected official's campaign committee, and subsequently 
expended for various purposes, retain their classification 
as campaign contributions and expenditures reportable unli 
under Chapter 4, Government Code Sections 84200, et seq.,_/ 
or become income to the elected official which is reportable 
by the official under Chapter 7, Sections 87200, et seq.~/ 
The specific purposes for which those funds received through 
the dinner sponsored by the Mayor Janet Gray Hayes Dinner 
Committee will be used are: 
(1) 
Hire a part-time assistant who will work to 
maintain the organization which supported the Mayor's cam-
paign; 
(2) 
Publish a newsletter from the Mayor; 
(3) 
Pay non-reimbursable travel expenses of the 
Mayor to conventions, conferences and meetings; 
(4) 
Purchase Mayor's trophies and awards; 
(5) 
Pay dinner expenses and travel costs not 
covered by the City of San Jose; 
(6) 
Pay travel expenses, postage and other expenses 
of the Mayor's volunteer assistants; and 
(7) 
Provide honoraria for future speakers for fund-
raisers. 
Those activities listed in (1), (2), (3), (4), (6) 
and (7) appear to be related to the Mayor's responsibilities 
as an elected official and future candidate, and have the 
effect of influencing future voter action. 
Accordingly, 
funds expended for these activities may properly be treated 
as campaign expenditures reportable by the Dinner Committee 
under Chapter 4 of the Political Reform Act. 
See opinion 
requested by Willie L. Brown, 1 FPPC Opinions ~7 (No. 75-055, 
July 2, 1975). 
1/ 
-All statutory references are to the Government 
Code unless otherwise noted. 
2/ 
~We assume, for purposes of this opinion, that the 
dinner was a political fundraiser and that the purchase of 
a ticket, therefore, was a contribution. 
See ~ection 82015. 


No. 75-145 
Page Three 
FPPC OPINIONS 
Funds ekpended for category (5), on the other hand, 
mayor may not be campaign expenditures reportable only 
under Chapter 4. 
If the expenses are incurred in connection 
with Ms. Hayes' political or governmental activities, they 
can be treated in the same manner as expenses incurred for 
the other activities sF~cified herein. 
If, however, the ex-
pellses are incurred to sUPP0rt personal dinner and travel 
costs of the Mayor, they will c~nstitute a 
ft to the Mayor 
from the Dinner Committee. 
Since gifts are included within 
the definition of income, Section 82030, the expenses will be 
reportable by the Dinner Cornmi ttee and also will be reportable 
by the Mayor on her Statement of Economic Interests filed 
pursuant to Chapter 7 of the Act (assuming, of course, that 
the. requisite amount of $25.00 or more is involved). 
Sections 
87203, 87207. 
Approved by the Commission on December 4, 1975. 
Concurring: 
Brosnahan, Carpenter, Lowenstein and Miller. 
Commissioner v-laters vIas absent. 
/2,~lJ/ LtUi-di2-
/Daniel H. Lowenstein 
Chairman
